summaryText: Guest Speaker Scott Williams opened with personal stories of travel delays and divine appointments, illustrating God's faithfulness to provide a seat at the table for those who trust Him. He then taught from the story of Mephibosheth in 2 Samuel 9, a man crippled by a circumstance he did not cause, who was living in isolation in Lo-debar. Pastor Scott explained that 'Lo-debar' represents a place where we lower our standards and settle for less than God's best, whether in relationships, finances, or mental health. The central message, titled 'Don't Lower the Bar,' offered three keys to move from a low place to the King's table: asking tough questions, giving honest answers, and doing what God says. He powerfully illustrated these points with the testimony of Ari Allen, a former basketball prodigy whose career was ended by an accident, showing how our condition does not define our position and how patient trust in God's process leads to purpose. The sermon concluded with a call to surrender to Jesus, emphasizing that God's grace restores and elevates us, setting us up for success even through setbacks.
mainPoints: To move from a place of settling (Lo-debar), we must be willing to ask ourselves tough questions about our lives and relationships.
Breakthrough requires giving honest answers, both to ourselves and to others, instead of living in denial or behind a facade.
Ultimate elevation comes from obeying God's direction, trusting that His process—even when difficult—leads to prosperity and purpose.

versesMentioned.text: David asked, 'Is there anyone still left of the house of Saul to whom I can show kindness for Jonathan’s sake?'... So Mephibosheth ate at David’s table like one of the king’s sons.

Monday.scripture: 2 Samuel 9:3 - 'The king asked, "Is there no one still alive from the house of Saul to whom I can show God’s kindness?"'
Monday.action: Identify one area of your life where you've been settling or avoiding a hard truth. Write down one honest, tough question you need to ask yourself about it.
Monday.prayer: God, give me the courage to ask the hard questions about my life. Help me to see clearly where I am and where You want me to be.

Tuesday.scripture: 2 Samuel 9:4 - 'Where is he?' the king asked. Ziba answered, 'He is at the house of Makir son of Ammiel in Lo Debar.'
Tuesday.action: Look in the mirror and speak an honest answer to the question you asked yesterday. Acknowledge your current 'Lo-debar' without sugarcoating it.
Tuesday.prayer: Lord, I choose honesty over comfort. Help me to acknowledge my real situation so I can receive Your real grace.

Wednesday.scripture: 2 Samuel 9:11 - 'Ziba said to the king, "Your servant will do whatever my lord the king commands his servant."'
Wednesday.action: Identify one specific thing you know God has been telling you to do (e.g., forgive someone, start a habit, end a habit). Take the first small step toward doing it today.
Wednesday.prayer: Father, I surrender my will to Yours. I choose to obey what You've shown me, trusting that Your way is best.

Thursday.scripture: Psalm 23:5 - 'You prepare a table before me in the presence of my enemies.'
Thursday.action: Recall a recent delay or disappointment. Write down one way you can choose to trust that God is setting a table for you in that situation.
Thursday.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You are preparing a place for me. I trust that You are working even in my setbacks for my good.

Friday.scripture: 2 Samuel 9:7 - 'You will always eat at my table.'
Friday.action: Declare your true identity out loud: 'I am a son/daughter of the King and have a seat at His table.' Repeat this whenever feelings of inadequacy arise.
Friday.prayer: God, remind me that my condition does not define my position. I am Your child, welcomed and valued at Your table.

Saturday.scripture: James 1:12 - 'Blessed is the one who perseveres under trial because, having stood the test, that person will receive the crown of life that the Lord has promised to those who love him.'
Saturday.action: Reflect on Ari's story or your own. Choose one difficult thing you will endure today, trusting it is part of God's process for your growth.
Saturday.prayer: Holy Spirit, grant me patience and endurance. Help me to trust Your process, knowing it leads to the purpose and prosperity You have for me.
